Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your HR system is secure, accessible, and connected to your other business systems. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA when handling employee data like NRIC and salary information, and you need role-based access so payroll officers cannot see recruitment files. It matters when your team works from multiple locations and needs to sync employee data with your accountant or government submissions like CPF.

Core HR.narrative: This is the backbone of your HR system — a single, authoritative record of every employee's personal details, contracts, and history. Singapore SMEs need this to stay compliant with MOM employment records requirements and to reduce manual paperwork. It matters when you onboard a new hire (ensuring all paperwork is captured) and when you need to retrieve an employee's contract or performance history quickly.

Time & Attendance.narrative: This family tracks when employees work, manages their leave, and calculates overtime — critical for payroll accuracy and labour law compliance. Singapore SMEs in retail, F&B, and logistics rely on this to roster shifts fairly, prevent overstaffing, and ensure leave balances are correct. It matters when you have shift workers or need to prove compliance with rest day rules, and when an employee disputes their overtime pay.

Payroll & Statutory.narrative: This family calculates salaries, deducts CPF and tax, generates payslips, and files mandatory returns to IRAS and MOM — the most regulated part of HR. Every Singapore SME must get this right or face penalties and employee disputes. It matters monthly when you run payroll, at year-end when you file IRAS AIS, and when a foreign worker's pass needs renewal.

Talent & Growth.narrative: This family helps you hire, develop, and retain your best people — from job posting through recruitment, performance reviews, training, and compensation planning. Singapore SMEs with high turnover or skill gaps benefit from structured hiring and training records to reduce hiring costs and build capability. It matters when you need to fill a critical role quickly, when you want to identify who is ready for promotion, or when you plan annual salary reviews.

Lifecycle & Analytics.narrative: This family gives HR leaders and managers visibility into headcount, turnover, hiring costs, and other key metrics through dashboards and reports. Singapore SMEs use this to spot trends — such as high attrition in a department — and make data-driven decisions on hiring and retention. It matters when you need to justify headcount to the board or when you want to understand why your hiring costs are rising.
